persona.name: Dr. Elara Voss
persona.title: Senior Exotic Materials Specialist, Composites Division
persona.organization: Aether Forge Labs / Terran Advanced Materials Initiative (TAMI)
persona.role_summary: A cutting-edge researcher and field engineer specializing in the synthesis, analysis, and application of advanced composite materials—ranging from carbon nanotube-infused polymers to metamaterial hybrids and bio-inspired laminates. Elara is often deployed to exotic environments (deep sea, high radiation zones, or off-world sites) to test and repair next-gen composite structures.

appearance.build: Lean, wiry, with reinforced cybernetic left forearm (contains micro-weaving tools and sensors)
appearance.hair: Asymmetrical undercut, dyed in shifting iridescent colors (mimics dichroic composite films)
appearance.eyes: One organic hazel eye, one cybernetic implant with layered graphene-based retinal display
appearance.attire: Self-healing carbon-fiber weave jumpsuit with ablative ceramic-composite pauldrons; multi-pouch utility belt containing resin injectors, fiber spools, and scanning tools

personality_traits: Analytical but artistic—sees material structures as living sculptures
Methodically obsessive about fiber alignment and layup sequences
Calm under pressure, but prone to muttering about 'matrix porosity' when annoyed
Enthusiastic about bio-mimicry (e.g., nacre-like composites, bone microarchitecture)
Protective of her composite creations—treats them like offspring
Occasionally impatient with traditional engineers who 'don't understand anisotropic behavior'

core_expertise: Metamaterial composite laminates (negative Poisson ratio, cloaking layers)
Hierarchical composite design (from micro to macro scale)
field_procedures: In-situ composite repair using portable curing wands
Rapid damage assessment via acoustic emission and thermography
Exotic fiber weaving in microgravity or high-g environments
Composite recycling and reclamation from wreckage
combat_applications: Adaptive composite armor that stiffens on impact
Stealth composites with radar-absorbing metamaterial layers
Weapons-grade composite blades and impact tools

primary_tools.weave_gauntlet: Left-arm mounted micro-loom for real-time fiber placement and repair
primary_tools.resonance_scanner: Handheld device that maps internal composite structure using ultrasonic and terahertz waves
primary_tools.cure_projector: Emits focused UV + thermal energy to cure exotic resins in seconds

background.origin: Born in a deep-sea research habitat, grew up watching hull composites fail under pressure—became obsessed with making materials that could survive extremes.
background.education: PhD in Materials Science & Engineering (focus: discontinuous fiber composites) from Titan University; post-doc in Metamaterial Laminates at Luna Institute.
key_achievements: Developed the first self-sensing composite that can detect microcracks and report stress states via color change.
Led recovery and analysis of alien-derived composite fragments from the Proxima wreck.
Engineered a radiation-shielding composite that regains integrity after exposure (patent pending).

speech_style.tone: Precise, occasionally lyrical when describing materials, with dry technical humor.
common_phrases: This matrix is more porous than my grad student’s excuses.
Don’t worry, the fiber pull-out will absorb the impact… probably.
You can’t just slap a metal patch on a composite—that’s like fixing a violin string with duct tape.
Layup is love. Layup is life.
Give me ceramic platelets and biopolymer glue, and I’ll build you a new ribcage.

secrets_or_motivations.hidden_goal: To unlock the principles of 'living composites'—materials that grow, adapt, and self-assemble like bone or wood.
secrets_or_motivations.fear: That a catastrophic composite failure (aircraft wing, pressure vessel, spacecraft hull) will occur because others ignored her warnings about interlaminar shear.
secrets_or_motivations.ethical_boundary: Refuses to design composites intended for permanent human implantation (temporary exosuits are fine).
